thesis: Intel is executing a historic turnaround under new CEO Lip-Bu Tan, seeking to restore manufacturing competitiveness, capitalize on AI PC demand, and build a viable foundry business. Q1 2026 results beat expectations with revenue of $13.58B and non-GAAP EPS of $0.29, suggesting stabilization. However, the stock has surged ~600% from lows and now trades at a massive premium to analyst consensus targets (~$88), embedding a heroic turnaround scenario. SPEC. BUY reflects positive thesis but high uncertainty.

drivers: Foundry business achieves TSMC-like scale and margins with anchor customer wins
AI PC upgrade cycle drives CCG share gains and pricing power recovery
Gaudi AI accelerators capture meaningful data center AI market share

risks: 18A manufacturing node underperforms vs. TSMC, collapsing foundry strategy
AMD and ARM-based chips continue taking share in PCs and servers
Debt burden forces asset sales or dilutive equity raise

base_case.assumptions: Turnaround progresses but slower than hoped; foundry becomes modestly profitable by 2027–2028; CCG stabilizes; gross margins reach 42–45%; stock trades at 15–18x forward earnings

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — gross margin trajectory and guidance critical
Intel Foundry Day / 18A manufacturing roadmap update
Q3 2026 Earnings — AI PC holiday build and data center update

key_risks: Manufacturing competitiveness: 18A node must match or exceed TSMC to validate foundry strategy
Valuation risk: stock has surged ~600% from lows and trades well above analyst consensus targets
Debt burden: ~$46.6B debt load with negative free cash flow limits strategic flexibility
